en.text: If the chapter on the measure phase seemed especially long, it’s because the phase itself is long and requires a great deal of work. Without a strong measure phase, the team cannot move on to analyze data and make data-based decisions that drive improve and control phases. Analyze phases also require a lot of work, but that work is usually performed by Black Belts and Green Belts, who report findings to the Six Sigma team and ask for feedback about analysis and verification of analysis.

In this chapter, we’ll discuss a number of tools that might be used by Six Sigma teams during the analyze phase, but we’ll also reference other chapters and units. Units 4, 5, 7 and 8 provide in-depth information about the statistical tools referenced throughout this chapter.

zh.text: 如果关于测量阶段的章节看起来特别长，那是因为该阶段本身很长并且需要大量工作。如果没有强有力的衡量阶段，团队就无法继续分析数据并做出基于数据的决策来推动改进和控制阶段。分析阶段也需要大量工作，但这些工作通常由黑带人员和绿带人员执行，他们向六西格玛团队报告结果，并要求分析和验证分析的反馈。
在本章中，我们将讨论六西格玛团队在分析阶段可能使用的一些工具，但我们也会参考其他章节和单元。第 4、5、7 和 8 单元提供了有关本章引用的统计工具的深入信息。
根本原因分析

en.text: One of the fundamental activities of the analyze phase performed by the entire team with help from identified subject matter experts is the root cause analysis. Root cause analysis is used to identify root causes for problems or defects when a team has reached the analyze phase without a clear idea of primary causation. Some of the tools described for identifying root causes in this chapter could be used in either define or measure phases at the discretion of the Black Belt leading the team; the FEMA described in Chapter 13 on measure could likewise be used in the analyze phase as part of root cause analysis.
The Cause and Effect, or Fishbone, Diagram

A popular method for brainstorming and analyzing causation in a process is the fishbone diagram. The fishbone diagram can be completed by a single Six Sigma expert, but it typically has more value when it is completed by the entire Six Sigma team. The diagram lets teams concentrate on a brainstorming process that generates ideas about possible problem causes, organizes those possibilities in a logical way, and lets teams visualize the information to identify priorities, trends, and relationships between ideas. When used as a team activity, the fishbone diagram encourages participation and input from all team members, which increases the chance of laying the foundational work for a viable and original solution.

The cause and effect diagram is called the fishbone diagram because you begin with what looks like a simple drawing of a fish skeleton. Reference the diagram below and follow the instructions to create a fishbone diagram as part of a team brainstorming exercise. You can also use these instructions now to practice a fishbone diagram based on a process or problem you have experience with.

1. Sketch a basic fishbone shape on a whiteboard or large flipchart. Write a summarized version of the problem where the fish head should be. Note: You don’t have to conduct a fishbone diagram only on the problem statement from the define phase. Teams might also conduct the diagram on a specific defect or issue found during the define or measure phase. For example, if the problem statement discusses the amount of waste in a restaurant, the team might have discovered during the measure phase that bread is being thrown away at a rate higher than all other food items. One of the activities handled in the analyze phase might be a fishbone diagram specifically about the reason for so much bread waste. 2. Draw a fish spine and four major connectors. Label each connector as People, Process, Materials, and Procedure. Some Black Belts also include two other major connectors: Equipment and Environment. 3. Explain the categories of the fishbone diagram to the team. Note that there are some places, especially with particular processes, where the various categories will overlap. Some ideas generated by the team as they complete the fishbone diagram might fit in more than one category, and that’s okay.

a. People references anyone who carries out or interacts with a process. b. Process or machine refers to the process by which inputs become outputs. c. Procedure or method refers to the way things are done, whether by written documents or unwritten rules. d. Materials are the inputs, such as raw goods, into the process. e. Equipment includes the technology or machines required to handle the work. f. Environment is the immediate area surrounding the process. 4. Begin with each category on the fishbone diagram, asking the team how something in that category might be responsible for a problem or defect. 5. Use sticky notes to write down ideas and place them on the fishbone diagram so you can move ideas around later. You can also write directly on the diagram. 6. Couple cause-and-effect brainstorming with the 5 Whys exercise described in Chapter 6. For each branch of the fishbone diagram, ask “Why?” at least five times to ensure the most granular detail possible. 7. Once the team has run out of ideas for the first category, repeat steps 4 through 6 for all other categories.

8. Take some time as a team to review the diagram, discussing the placement of potential causes, and moving them to appropriate categories and subsections to create an organized visual representation. 9. Remove or cross-out causes that don’t prove to be valid after initial discussion. 10. As a team, decide which root causes seem most likely or highest priority. Circle those causes as high-priority possibilities for further investigation.
Cause and Effect Brainstorm Example

To provide a better idea of how a fishbone diagram works, consider the example image below and we’ll walk through how the team came up with the information recorded on the procedure/method line of this diagram.

The team above was working to solve a problem of burnt cakes in a food-service bakery. When discussing the method by which the cakes are being baked, the team first came up the with the reason that the cakes were being baked at inconsistent times. Perhaps, suggested one team member, staff weren’t paying attention and were leaving cakes in the oven too long. The idea was written down.

The instructions for baking cakes are vague, said another team member – this time, a subject matter expert from the bakery. “Why?” asked the team. The subject matter expert responded that the instructions in the bakery don’t take various types of cakes into account, leaving staff guessing about bake times for some cakes. Further “Why?” questions helped the team determine that new cakes were added to the menu without the overall instructions for bakery staff being updated.

After digging deeper into the inconsistent baking times, the team again asked themselves how methods could be responsible for burnt cakes. Someone suggested that the temperature in the oven was too hot, and the team tied that suggestion to the same root cause as the inconsistent bake times. Upon final review, someone noted that the suggestion that staff not paying attention was a cause wasn’t valid, because the bakery was equipped with alarms that sounded when baking time was done. The team crossed that idea off the diagram.

In this case, the Six Sigma team might prioritize the fact that instructions are not available for all types of items being prepared in the bakery. Because this would likely be a simple and common-sense improvement to make, the Black Belt might even assign someone to begin working on the improve

phase as soon as the cause was verified. Many times, the root cause is not as obvious and the solution for the cause even less obvious, requiring additional analysis and validation before moving forward.
Root Cause Verification Matrix

Once teams identify possible root causes, they must verify that the causes are valid. Root cause verification can be completed via a variety of methods, including statistical analysis, design of experiments, logical questioning, observing a process, gathering additional data, analyzing data via graphical representation, and mapping processes at a more granular level than accomplished in the define phase. While this chapter touches briefly on statistical analysis and graphical representation, those topics, as well as experiments and process mapping, are covered in later units.

Whatever method is used to validate root cause assumptions, the Six Sigma team should document it. Documentation regarding root cause verification is usually completed on a matrix that includes the problem, possible root causes, the verification method, why the verification method was chosen, results of the verification, and, in some cases, whether a senior Six Sigma leader, such as a Master Black Belt, agrees. A template for such a matrix is included below, but teams can also create similar documents in Excel or Word.

rows: 术语说明 Root Cause（根本原因）：真正驱动问题发生的原因；解决它才能防止问题反复出现。
zh.text: 术语说明 Root Cause（根本原因）：真正驱动问题发生的原因；解决它才能防止问题反复出现。
整个团队在确定的主题专家的帮助下执行的分析阶段的基本活动之一是根本原因分析。当团队进入分析阶段但不清楚主要原因时，根本原因分析用于识别问题或缺陷的根本原因。本章中描述的用于识别根本原因的一些工具可以由领导团队的黑带自行决定用于定义或测量阶段；第 13 章中关于措施的 FEMA 描述同样可以用于分析阶段，作为根本原因分析的一部分。
因果图或鱼骨图

rows: 术语说明 Fishbone / Ishikawa（鱼骨图）：按类别梳理潜在原因的根因分析工具。
zh.text: 术语说明 Fishbone / Ishikawa（鱼骨图）：按类别梳理潜在原因的根因分析工具。
集思广益和分析过程中的因果关系的一种流行方法是鱼骨图。鱼骨图可以由一个六西格玛高手完成，但是当整个六西格玛团队完成时，它通常具有更大的价值。该图让团队专注于集思广益的过程，该过程产生有关可能的问题原因的想法，以逻辑方式组织这些可能性，并让团队可视化信息以确定优先级、趋势和想法之间的关系。当用作团队活动时，鱼骨图鼓励所有团队成员的参与和投入，这增加了为可行且原创的解决方案奠定基础工作的机会。
因果图称为鱼骨图，因为您从看起来像鱼骨架的简单绘图开始。 参考下图并按照说明创建鱼骨图，作为团队头脑风暴练习的一部分。您现在还可以使用这些说明，根据您遇到过的流程或问题来练习鱼骨图。

1. 在白板或大活动挂图上画出基本的鱼骨形状。写出鱼头应该在哪里的问题的总结版本。注意：您不必仅对定义阶段的问题陈述进行鱼骨图。团队还可以针对定义或测量阶段发现的特定缺陷或问题绘制图表。例如，如果问题陈述讨论餐厅的浪费量，团队可能在测量阶段发现面包的丢弃率高于所有其他食品。分析阶段处理的活动之一可能是鱼骨图，专门说明大量面包浪费的原因。 2. 画出鱼刺和四个主要连接器。将每个连接器标记为人员、流程、材料和程序。一些黑带人员还包括另外两个主要连接器：设备和环境。 3. 向团队解释鱼骨图的类别。请注意，在某些地方，特别是在特定流程中，各种类别会重叠。团队在完成鱼骨图时产生的一些想法可能适合多个类别，这没关系。

a.人们指的是执行流程或与流程交互的任何人。 b.过程或机器是指输入变成输出的过程。 c.程序或方法是指完成事情的方式，无论是通过书面文件还是不成文的规则。 d.材料是流程的输入，例如原材料。 e.设备包括处理工作所需的技术或机器。 f.环境是过程周围的直接区域。 4. 从鱼骨图上的每个类别开始，询问团队该类别中的某些内容如何可能导致问题或缺陷。 5. 使用便利贴写下想法并将其放在鱼骨图上，以便以后可以移动想法。也可以直接在图上书写。 6. 将因果头脑风暴与第 6 章中描述的 5 个为什么练习结合起来。对于鱼骨图的每个分支，问“为什么？”至少五次以确保尽可能详细的细节。 7. 一旦团队耗尽了第一个类别的想法，就对所有其他类别重复步骤 4 到 6。

rows: 术语说明 5 Whys（五个为什么）：连续追问原因，帮助从表面症状深入到根本原因。
zh.text: 术语说明 5 Whys（五个为什么）：连续追问原因，帮助从表面症状深入到根本原因。
8. 花一些时间作为一个团队来审查图表，讨论潜在原因的位置，并将它们移至适当的类别和小节以创建有组织的视觉表示。 9. 删除或划掉经过初步讨论后证明无效的原因。 10. 作为一个团队，决定哪些根本原因最有可能或最优先。将这些原因圈出，作为进一步调查的高优先级可能性。
因果头脑风暴示例
为了更好地了解鱼骨图的工作原理，请考虑下面的示例图像，我们将逐步介绍团队如何得出记录在该图的过程/方法行上的信息。
上述团队正在努力解决一家食品服务面包店的蛋糕烧焦问题。在讨论蛋糕的烘焙方法时，团队首先提出了蛋糕烘焙时间不一致的原因。一位团队成员表示，也许是工作人员没有注意，将蛋糕放在烤箱里的时间太长了。这个想法被写下来了。
另一位团队成员（这次是面包店的主题专家）表示，烘焙蛋糕的说明含糊不清。 “为什么？”团队问道。该主题专家回应说，面包店的说明没有考虑到各种类型的蛋糕，导致工作人员猜测某些蛋糕的烘烤时间。进一步“为什么？”这些问题帮助团队确定新蛋糕已添加到菜单中，但面包店工作人员的总体说明并未更新。
在深入研究不一致的烘焙时间后，团队再次问自己，方法是如何导致蛋糕烧焦的。有人建议烤箱内的温度太高，团队将该建议与烘烤时间不一致的根本原因联系起来。经过最终审查，有人指出，工作人员不注意是原因的说法并不成立，因为面包店配备了烘烤时间结束时响起的警报。团队把这个想法从图表中划掉了。
在这种情况下，六西格玛团队可能会优先考虑以下事实：面包店中准备的所有类型的物品都无法获得说明。 因为这可能是一个简单且常识性的改进，黑带甚至可能会指派专人开始进行改进
原因查明后立即进入阶段。很多时候，根本原因并不那么明显，解决方案也不太明显，需要在继续之前进行额外的分析和验证。
根本原因验证矩阵
一旦团队确定了可能的根本原因，他们必须验证这些原因是否有效。根本原因验证可以通过多种方法完成，包括统计分析、实验设计、逻辑提问、观察过程、收集附加数据、通过图形表示分析数据，以及比定义阶段更精细地映射过程。虽然本章简要介绍了统计分析和图形表示，但这些主题以及实验和流程图将在后面的单元中介绍。

rows: 术语说明 DOE（实验设计）：系统安排实验以识别因素、水平和交互作用对结果的影响。
zh.text: 术语说明 DOE（实验设计）：系统安排实验以识别因素、水平和交互作用对结果的影响。
无论使用什么方法来验证根本原因假设，六西格玛团队都应该记录下来。有关根本原因验证的文件通常是在一个矩阵上完成的，其中包括问题、可能的根本原因、验证方法、为什么选择验证方法、验证结果，以及在某些情况下，六西格玛高级领导（例如黑带大师）是否同意。下面包含了此类矩阵的模板，但团队也可以在 Excel 或 Word 中创建类似的文档。
已验证？注释
问题

验证方法
原因 验证方法
烧焦的蛋糕示例的根本原因验证矩阵可能如下完成：

en.text: Six Sigma experts and teams can use a variety of graphical analysis tools to help generate ideas about root causes or understand how inputs and outputs really impact each other. Some of those graphical analysis tools require statistical analysis software, and those will be covered in later chapters. In this section, we’ll look at a few graphical representations you can create easily with Excel.
Pareto Chart

The first graphical tool for validating root causes is the Pareto chart, which was covered in chapter 5. Chapter 5 discussed the Pareto Principle, or 80/20 rule, which says that 20 percent of the causes lead to 80 percent of the results. Because of this, a Pareto chart is a good starting point for root cause brainstorming – teams can start with the few inputs or attributes accounting for the bulk of the Pareto chart. Just as you can “drill down” using the fishbone diagram, asking deeper and deeper “Why?” questions, you can drill down using a Pareto chart.
Consider the Pareto chart illustrating reasons for medical claims denials from Chapter 5.

In this case, the team might decide to concentrate on the top two claims denial reasons: duplicate claim and timely filing. The team could use various methods for digging deeper into root causes for these two claims denial reasons. They might perform a fishbone diagram to discover why duplicate claims are being generated. To understand the timely filing problem, teams might gather additional data for graphical analysis.

Timely filing means that the claim was not originally filed with an insurance company prior to the deadline for claims submission. Different insurance companies have various timely filing requirements, and the countdown usually starts at the time of service to the patient or the time of discharge from a facility. The team might want to understand which payers are denying claims for timely filing, so they collect data as follows on how many timely filing denials are associated with each payer. Because Pareto analysis is concerned with the top few, you can lump the many others together in a single entry and, for the purposes of the Pareto analysis, ignore them. A medical provider might bill claims to dozens of providers; including every provider on the data table and Pareto chart would be a waste of both time and space for this particular exercise.
Payer Denials A 3512 B 2779 C 1575 D 1142 E 945 F 847 G 702 H 502 Others 1241
Converted to a basic Pareto chart, the data is illustrated in the graph below.

It’s easy to see from the graph that the bulk of the problem is with payers A and B; perhaps these companies have shorter timely filing guidelines than the other companies or billing staff is unaware of the proper timely filing requirements for those payers. Six Sigma teams can begin asking questions specific to these payers as they continue analyzing data and discovering root causes.
Box Plots

Box plots are another graphical representation that can be handled with Excel. In later chapters on statistical analysis, we’ll cover how box plots can be related to hypothesis testing and other analysis. When differences between distributions are marked, however, or when outliers are fairly obvious within data, the image of a box plot tells its own story without requiring advanced statistical knowledge.

Box plots are often called Box-and-Whisker graphs. To understand how to read a box plot, consider the data table and graph below. The data table shows the time in minutes in which various runners completed a one-mile race. The results are divided into the categories Children and Adults.

The above image shows a box plot of the data table, generated in Excel. Even without further explanation, you can likely tell that the children completed the race on average slower than the adults. The blue box, which represents the children, is shorter than the orange box, indicating that the middle 50 percent of children completed the mile-long run in times that had less variation than that of the middle 50 percent of adults. To understand how this conclusion was arrived at, we’ll take a look at all the elements of a box plot.

A box plot begins with the upper and lower hinge – the top and bottom of the box. The top represents the 75th percentile; the bottom represents the 25th percentile of the data. The line within the box represents the 50th percentile. Within the box are the 50 percent of data points between the 25th and 75th percentiles.

Each box plot receives upper and lower whiskers indicating the range of most of the other data within a set. In this case, Excel creates whiskers that extend to the top and bottom of a range barring any
statistical outliers. Some statistical analysis software or methodologies use other methods to create the whiskers with very similar results.

Finally, since all plot points must be represented on a box plot graph, outliers are indicated with dots. You’ll see a blue dot above the children’s box, representing the data point of 29 minutes. That particular point is a statistical outlier; Six Sigma teams who note outliers on box plots should consider the data that is shown as an outlier. If an explanation can be found for the outlier, it can be ignored. For example, if the child who took 29 minutes to complete the mile was much younger than the other children or was walking with an injury, the data point is explained and can be excluded from further analysis.

In addition to calling out outliers, box plots let you compare two distributions graphically to see if, as in the above example, there are obvious differences between the data sets. Box plots are useful in comparing how various attributes impact a process. Six Sigma teams might compare process results for different operators, different times of day, different teams, or using different inputs. It’s important when comparing data in this fashion to only alter one attribute or input; otherwise, you won’t be able to tell what the cause of any difference between data sets was if a statistical difference does seem likely on a box plot.
Use some information for a work process you are familiar with, or use the sample data provided, to create box plots in Excel following the steps provided below.

A department manager believes that the staff on her teams would be more productive if they were able to work with two computer monitors. Because outfitting an entire department with dual monitors would be costly, the manager’s boss requires some proof that her assumption is correct. The manager equips a few stations with dual monitors and lets different team members work at the stations. She records the amount of work done within hourly increments at stations that have dual monitors as well as stations that have single monitors. Her data is featured in the table below.
One
Monitor
Create a box plot of the information in the manager’s data table.
1. Copy the data from the table above into Excel. 2. Highlight all of the data cells, including the header row. 3. Click Insert > Statistic Chart > More Statistical Charts
4. Select Box and Whisker and click OK.
5. Using normal Excel chart editing functions, edit the title and data labels of your chart as desired.

While a Six Sigma Black Belt would be able to back up the conclusion with hypothesis testing or other analytics, the manager might get her request for monitors approved with nothing more than this box plot. It’s easy to see that the second monitor did increase production capability for staff. Another thing worth noting is that the distributions for each of the boxes and whiskers is similar, which is somewhat expected. High producers are still going to produce the most, and low producers are still going to produce less than high produces, even if everyone is producing slightly more with the new set-up.

Note that the two examples used in this chapter used data sets that were different enough to be visually noticeable on a box plot. This isn’t always the case, which is why box plots and other graphical representations are often only the starting point for analysis.

zh.text: 工艺流程
观察
使用说明
不提供给工作人员
确定面包店工作人员是否有完成无缺陷工作所需的指示的简单方法
图形分析
六西格玛的专家和团队可以使用各种图形分析工具来帮助产生有关根本原因的想法或了解输入和输出如何真正相互影响。其中一些图形分析工具需要统计分析软件，这些将在后面的章节中介绍。在本节中，我们将介绍一些可以使用 Excel 轻松创建的图形表示形式。
帕累托图

rows: 术语说明 Pareto（帕累托）：按影响大小排序，常用于找出“少数关键原因”。
zh.text: 术语说明 Pareto（帕累托）：按影响大小排序，常用于找出“少数关键原因”。
第一个用于验证根本原因的图形工具是帕累托图，它在第 5 章中进行了介绍。第 5 章讨论了帕累托原理，或 80/20 规则，该规则表示 20% 的原因导致 80% 的结果。因此，帕累托图是根本原因头脑风暴的良好起点——团队可以从占帕累托图大部分的少数输入或属性开始。就像你可以使用鱼骨图“向下钻取”一样，越来越深入地问“为什么？”问题，您可以使用帕累托图进行深入分析。
考虑第 5 章中说明拒绝医疗索赔原因的帕累托图。
在这种情况下，团队可能会决定专注于索赔被拒绝的两个最重要原因：重复索赔和及时提交。该团队可以使用各种方法来深入挖掘这两个索赔被拒绝的根本原因。他们可能会执行鱼骨图来发现为什么会生成重复的索赔。为了了解及时归档问题，团队可能会收集额外的数据进行图形分析。

及时提交意味着索赔最初并未在索赔提交截止日期之前向保险公司提出。不同的保险公司有不同的及时归档要求，倒计时通常从为患者提供服务或出院时开始。 该团队可能想了解哪些付款人拒绝及时提交索赔，因此他们收集以下数据，了解与每个付款人相关的及时提交拒绝的数量。由于帕累托分析关注的是前几个，因此您可以将许多其他项集中在一个条目中，并且出于帕累托分析的目的，忽略它们。一家医疗服务提供者可能会向数十家服务提供者收取理赔费用；对于这个特定的练习来说，将每个提供商都包含在数据表和帕累托图中会浪费时间和空间。
转换为基本帕累托图后，数据如下图所示。
从图中很容易看出，大部分问题出在付款人 A 和 B 身上；也许这些公司的及时申报指南比其他公司更短，或者计费人员不知道这些付款人的适当及时申报要求。 六西格玛团队可以在继续分析数据和发现根本原因的同时，开始询问这些付款人的具体问题。
箱线图
箱线图是另一种可以用 Excel 处理的图形表示形式。在后面有关统计分析的章节中，我们将介绍箱形图如何与假设检验和其他分析相关。然而，当分布之间的差异被标记出来时，或者当数据中的异常值相当明显时，箱形图的图像就会讲述自己的故事，而无需高级的统计知识。

rows: 术语说明 Hypothesis Test（假设检验）：用样本数据判断某个统计假设是否有足够证据被拒绝。
zh.text: 术语说明 Hypothesis Test（假设检验）：用样本数据判断某个统计假设是否有足够证据被拒绝。
箱线图通常称为箱须图。要了解如何阅读箱线图，请考虑下面的数据表和图表。数据表显示了不同跑步者完成一英里比赛的时间（以分钟为单位）。结果分为儿童和成人类别。
上图显示了在 Excel 中生成的数据表的箱线图。即使没有进一步的解释，您也可以看出孩子们完成比赛的平均速度比成年人慢。 代表儿童的蓝色框比橙色框短，表明中间 50% 的儿童完成一英里长跑的时间比中间 50% 的成人的变化要小。为了理解这个结论是如何得出的，我们将看一下箱形图的所有元素。
箱形图从上铰链和下铰链（盒子的顶部和底部）开始。顶部代表第 75 个百分位；底部代表数据的第 25 个百分位。方框内的线代表第 50 个百分位数。框中包含第 25 个百分位数和第 75 个百分位数之间的 50% 的数据点。
每个箱线图都有上下须线，指示一组内大多数其他数据的范围。在这种情况下，Excel 创建的须线延伸到范围的顶部和底部，禁止任何
统计异常值。一些统计分析软件或方法使用其他方法来创建具有非常相似结果的晶须。
最后，由于所有绘图点都必须在箱形图上表示，因此异常值用点表示。您会在儿童框上方看到一个蓝点，代表 29 分钟的数据点。该特定点是统计异常值； 在箱线图上标注异常值的六西格玛团队应考虑显示为异常值的数据。如果可以找到异常值的解释，则可以忽略它。例如，如果用 29 分钟完成一英里的孩子比其他孩子年轻得多，或者走路时受伤，则对数据点进行解释，并可以将其排除在进一步分析之外。
除了标出异常值之外，箱线图还可以让您以图形方式比较两个分布，以查看数据集之间是否存在明显差异（如上例所示）。箱线图可用于比较各种属性如何影响过程。 六西格玛团队可能会比较不同操作员、一天中不同时间、不同团队或使用不同输入的流程结果。以这种方式比较数据时，仅更改一个属性或输入非常重要；否则，如果箱线图上确实存在统计差异，您将无法判断数据集之间存在差异的原因是什么。
使用您熟悉的工作流程的一些信息，或使用提供的示例数据，按照下面提供的步骤在 Excel 中创建箱线图。

rows: 术语说明 Sample / Sampling（样本/抽样）：从总体中选取部分数据进行分析；样本应尽量代表总体。
zh.text: 术语说明 Sample / Sampling（样本/抽样）：从总体中选取部分数据进行分析；样本应尽量代表总体。
一位部门经理认为，如果她的团队的员工能够使用两台计算机显示器工作，他们的工作效率将会更高。由于为整个部门配备双显示器成本高昂，因此经理的老板需要一些证据来证明她的假设是正确的。经理为几个工作站配备了双显示器，并让不同的团队成员在这些工作站上工作。她记录了具有双显示器的工作站和具有单显示器的工作站以每小时增量完成的工作量。她的数据如下表所示。
一
监视器
创建经理数据表中信息的箱线图。
1. 将上表中的数据复制到 Excel 中。 2. 突出显示所有数据单元格，包括标题行。 3. 单击“插入”>“统计图表”>“更多统计图表”
4. 选择“盒子和晶须”，然后单击“确定”。
5. 使用普通的 Excel 图表编辑功能，根据需要编辑图表的标题和数据标签。
虽然六西格玛黑带能够通过假设检验或其他分析来支持结论，但经理可能只需要这个箱形图就可以批准她对监视器的请求。很容易看出第二台显示器确实提高了员工的生产能力。另一件值得注意的事情是，每个盒子和晶须的分布是相似的，这在某种程度上是预期的。高生产商的产量仍将最多，而低生产商的产量仍将低于高生产商，即使每个人在新设置下生产的产量略有增加。
请注意，本章中使用的两个示例使用的数据集差异很大，可以在箱线图上直观地看到。情况并非总是如此，这就是为什么箱线图和其他图形表示通常只是分析的起点。
统计分析
由于统计分析将在以后的几个单元中介绍，因此本节列出了一些常见的统计分析工具及其定义。

en.text: Because statistical analysis is covered in several future units, this section lists some common statistical analysis tools with definitions.
Hypothesis Testing

Hypothesis testing lets Six Sigma experts draw conclusions about the population based on statistical analysis performed on a sample. Because the conclusions are based on samples and not the entire population, there is always some risk of error. You might have seen or heard poll results given with a plus/minus in the result: “60 percent, plus or minus 2 percentage points, would vote for the candidate today.” That plus/minus is the value for the error risk.

In statistical analysis, the risk that a sample doesn’t offer a good representation of the population is known as the alpha-risk and the beta-risk. Using information about the sample and alpha and beta risks,
statisticians calculate what is called the p-value. The p-value is a probability estimate that tells statisticians how likely an assumption or conclusion drawn on sample data will be incorrect.

Statistical software removes a lot of the manual calculations from the process of setting up and running hypothesis tests. With Minitab, for example, Six Sigma experts can conduct hypothesis tests on prepared data with a few mouse clicks. They do have to know which types of hypothesis tests to use in which situations.
Correlation and Regression Analysis

Regression and correlation analysis helps Six Sigma experts understand how variables within a process might be related. Regression analysis helps teams define the relationship between one independent variable – possibly an input – and one dependent variable – possibly an output. Does the temperature in the oven have a relationship to whether the cake is baked correctly, and how close are the two things related? Does the number of hours a person works have an impact on his or her productivity – can the team show a correlation between lower production as employees approach the end of a shift? These are the types of questions that regression analysis can answer.

To work with regression analysis, both of the variables being studied have to be in numerical format. To conduct a regression analysis regarding the relationship between oven temperature and whether a cake is baked correctly, a Six Sigma team baked cakes at varying temperatures and rated them numerically on “doneness.” A rating of 1 indicated the cake hardly cooked at all; a rating of 5 indicated a perfectly baked cake. At 10, the cake was completely burned. The temperatures and corresponding ratings are seen in the data table below.
Using this data, the team creates a scatter diagram with a trend line, as seen below.

Just looking at this graph, you can tell that a relationship does exist. The data plots are gathered tightly around the trend line, which indicates that as temperature rises so does the doneness of the cake.
Design of Experiments

Correlation and regression analysis doesn’t always provide the information a team needs to determine relationships between variables, especially when those relationships are complex, or multiple variables are present. Because the analyze phase sets the stage for the improve phase, teams have to be as certain as possible in their analytical conclusions before they decide on solutions for implementation. A design of experiments can provide the more granular details and analysis required for that level of certainty.
Design of experiments, or DoE, is performed via statistical analysis software such as Minitab. Teams can set up experiments for one factor or multiple factors.

zh.text: 假设检验让六西格玛专家根据对样本进行的统计分析得出有关总体的结论。由于结论是基于样本而不是整个总体，因此总是存在一定的错误风险。您可能已经看到或听到过带有正/负的民意调查结果：“60%，正负 2 个百分点，今天会投票给候选人。”该正/负就是错误风险的值。

rows: 术语说明 Population（总体）：研究对象的全部集合；实际项目中常通过样本推断总体。
zh.text: 术语说明 Population（总体）：研究对象的全部集合；实际项目中常通过样本推断总体。
在统计分析中，样本不能很好地代表总体的风险称为 α 风险和 β 风险。使用有关样本以及 alpha 和 beta 风险的信息，
统计学家计算所谓的 p 值。 p 值是一种概率估计，它告诉统计学家根据样本数据得出的假设或结论不正确的可能性有多大。

rows: 术语说明 p-value（p 值）：在原假设成立时观察到当前或更极端结果的概率；常用于判断显著性。
zh.text: 术语说明 p-value（p 值）：在原假设成立时观察到当前或更极端结果的概率；常用于判断显著性。
统计软件消除了设置和运行假设检验过程中的大量手动计算。例如，使用 Minitab，六西格玛专家只需点击几下鼠标即可对准备好的数据进行假设检验。他们必须知道在哪种情况下使用哪种类型的假设检验。
相关性和回归分析

rows: 术语说明 Correlation（相关）：描述两个变量共同变化的程度，不直接证明因果关系。
zh.text: 术语说明 Correlation（相关）：描述两个变量共同变化的程度，不直接证明因果关系。

rows: 术语说明 Regression（回归）：用模型估计变量之间的关系，并预测响应变量。
zh.text: 术语说明 Regression（回归）：用模型估计变量之间的关系，并预测响应变量。
回归和相关分析帮助六西格玛专家了解流程中的变量如何相关。回归分析帮助团队定义一个自变量（可能是输入）和一个因变量（可能是输出）之间的关系。烤箱内的温度与蛋糕是否正确烘烤有关系吗？两者的关系有多密切？一个人的工作时数是否会影响他或她的生产力？团队能否显示出员工临近轮班结束时生产力下降之间的相关性？这些是回归分析可以回答的问题类型。
为了进行回归分析，所研究的两个变量都必须采用数字格式。为了对烤箱温度与蛋糕烘焙是否正确之间的关系进行回归分析，六西格玛团队在不同的温度下烘焙蛋糕，并对蛋糕的“熟度”进行数字评分。 1 分表示蛋糕根本没有煮熟； 5 分表示蛋糕烘焙完美。 10点的时候，蛋糕完全烧焦了。 温度和相应的额定值见下面的数据表。
使用这些数据，团队创建了带有趋势线的散点图，如下所示。

rows: 术语说明 Scatter Diagram（散点图）：展示两个变量之间关系的图形；相关不等于因果。
zh.text: 术语说明 Scatter Diagram（散点图）：展示两个变量之间关系的图形；相关不等于因果。
只要看一下这张图，您就可以看出确实存在某种关系。数据图紧密聚集在趋势线周围，这表明随着温度升高，蛋糕的熟度也会升高。
实验设计
相关性和回归分析并不总能提供团队确定变量之间关系所需的信息，尤其是当这些关系复杂或存在多个变量时。由于分析阶段为改进阶段奠定了基础，因此团队在决定实施解决方案之前必须尽可能确定其分析结论。实验设计可以提供该确定性级别所需的更精细的细节和分析。
实验设计 (DOE) 通过 Minitab 等统计分析软件进行。团队可以针对一个因素或多个因素设置实验。

en.text: o Primary root causes have been identified. o Team has prioritized root causes. o Champion or sponsor agrees with team priorities moving into the improve phase. o Where possible, root cause assumptions are backed by statistical data. o Relationships between variables within a process are understood. o Where possible, variable relationships have been confirmed with statistical analysis.

zh.text: o 主要根本原因已确定。 o 团队已优先考虑根本原因。 o 冠军或发起人同意进入改进阶段的团队优先事项。 o 在可能的情况下，根本原因假设有统计数据支持。 o 理解流程中变量之间的关系。 o 在可能的情况下，变量关系已通过统计分析得到确认。

en.text: During the improve phase of a DMAIC project, Six Sigma teams brainstorm possible solutions for the root causes identified in the analyze phase and rank those solutions according to costs, how effective the solution would be, and how likely the solution could be implemented. Analytical rankings are used to prioritize and select solutions for implementation. Teams pilot solutions through beta tests or small roll outs, collect data on the solution, and verify that the solution is working as expected via statistical analysis. Once the team is confident that the solution works to address the problem, it plans and implements a full rollout of the solution.

zh.text: 在DMAIC项目的改进阶段，六西格玛团队针对分析阶段确定的根本原因集思广益，讨论可能的解决方案，并根据成本、解决方案的有效性以及解决方案实施的可能性对这些解决方案进行排名。分析排名用于确定优先级并选择要实施的解决方案。团队通过 Beta 测试或小规模推广来试用解决方案，收集解决方案的数据，并通过统计分析验证解决方案是否按预期工作。一旦团队确信该解决方案能够解决问题，就会计划并实施该解决方案的全面部署。

rows: 术语说明 DMAIC：定义、测量、分析、改进、控制；适用于改进已有流程。
zh.text: 术语说明 DMAIC：定义、测量、分析、改进、控制；适用于改进已有流程。

rows: 术语说明 Root Cause（根本原因）：真正驱动问题发生的原因；解决它才能防止问题反复出现。
zh.text: 术语说明 Root Cause（根本原因）：真正驱动问题发生的原因；解决它才能防止问题反复出现。
解决方案选择矩阵

en.text: A solutions selection matrix is an analytical tool that lets teams propose and rank solutions for any of the root causes identified in the analyze phase. While teams can work simultaneously on multiple solutions if multiple prioritized root causes were found, Black Belt and other team leaders must remain cognizant of timelines, resources, scope, and the purpose of the project. If a single solution provides enough positive impact to reach project goals, then other solutions might be saved for future improvement projects. If one solution would reach results, but another solution would be especially easy to implement and provide additional positive results, the team is likely to decide to implement both.

A solutions selection matrix can be created in Excel, and teams should work on the document together in a brainstorming capacity. It’s a good idea to include the entire team as well as relevant subject matter experts and stakeholders during solutions brainstorming. This ensures the solutions the team comes up with are more likely to be a realistic fit for the process and business; once solutions are selected using the matrix, teams will also likely have to get sponsor, champion, or leadership council support before a partial or full implementation is possible. This is especially true where solutions require expenditure or will impact processes and people outside of the project’s scope.
An example solutions selection matrix is pictured below.
The matrix is completed by:
1. Entering the problem statement in the first column. This should be the final problem statement that was arrived at during the measure phase if the team decided that the statement should be

altered after gathering data. Otherwise, this can be the problem statement from the define phase. 2. Entering a priority validated root cause from the analyze phase. If the team is going to attempt to solve more than one root cause during the improve phase, it should create a solutions selection matrix for each root cause. 3. Brainstorming potential solutions in column three. During the brainstorming process, teams should not question or attempt to analyze solutions, but should record any solution suggested that seems at all viable. The only solutions that might be ignored are those that are clearly out of scope or impossible, but the Black Belt leading the brainstorming exercise will have to use his or her own discernment about such suggestions. 4. Noting, at a very high level, the practical methods by which a solution could be implemented. In one example used in the analyze chapter, a commercial bakery had a problem that was caused by lack of baking instructions. In this case, the Six Sigma team might propose that staff be provided with proper instructions. The method by which that solution occurs is written documentation and training. 5. Rating solutions. After a list of possible solutions and practical methods is created, the team rates each possibility on effectiveness, feasibility, and cost-benefit. Each category is given a rating between 1 and 10.

a. Effectiveness is the measure of how well a solution will eliminate a root cause for a problem, with 1 being not effective and 10 being highly effective. b. Feasibility is the measure of effort required to implement the improvement, with 1 being not feasible because of the effort or resources required and 10 being highly feasible. c. Cost-benefit is an estimated measure of how the costs of a project compare to the savings expected. This rating is not a formal cost benefit analysis, but is a high-level estimation. If savings are expected to outweigh the costs associated with a project, the team ranks the solution as high. Otherwise, the team ranks the solution low. 6. The scores for effectiveness, feasibility, and cost-benefit are multiplied to calculate an overall score. The overall score can be used to prioritize solutions and select the solution that features the best overall effectiveness, feasibility, and cost-benefit rating.
Consider a possible solutions selection matrix created regarding the medical claims denial example used in the analyze chapter.

During the analyze chapter, a Pareto analysis indicated that timely filing issues were a root cause for high claims denials. In the solutions selection matrix above, a team has identified and prioritized four possible solutions after discovering that claims were not filed on time because claims billing staff didn’t have all the proper information in time.

The first solution is to require the front desk to collect as much information as possible. The team gave this solution a mid-range rating for effectiveness because it doesn’t address the fact that clinical information can still be missing from the claim. But, the process would be effective at gathering demographic information and would not require large expense or effort to implement, so the team rated it high for the other categories.

Next, the team considered creating and requiring a patient email or portal system so billing staff could communicate quickly with patients when information was missing. Because not all patients have email and many would be unlikely to use such a system, the team ranked this solution as low for feasibility and effectiveness.

The third solution considered by the team was requiring clinicians to include all information necessary for billing as they charted during the visit with the patient. The team thought this solution would be fairly effective, but that clinicians would be unlikely to take the time to be so thorough when dealing with patient issues. The solution might also slow physician visits, resulting in a negative impact to revenue.

Finally, the team considered a solution that put certain staff members in charge of claims with missing information. Because those staff members could concentrate on missing claims and would work through a new workflow built by the team, they would be more likely to file claims on time. Overall, the team
ranked this solution highest, but they decided to implement both solutions one and four because solution one required so little effort and would actually help drive the success of solution four.

zh.text: 解决方案选择矩阵是一种分析工具，可让团队针对分析阶段确定的任何根本原因提出解决方案并对其进行排名。虽然如果发现多个优先根本原因，团队可以同时处理多个解决方案，但黑带和其他团队领导必须始终了解项目的时间表、资源、范围和目的。如果单个解决方案提供了足够的积极影响来实现项目目标，那么可能会保留其他解决方案以供将来的改进项目使用。如果一种解决方案能够取得成果，但另一种解决方案特别容易实施并提供额外的积极成果，那么团队可能会决定同时实施这两种解决方案。

rows: 术语说明 Scope（项目范围）：说明改进项目包括什么、不包括什么，避免目标扩张。
zh.text: 术语说明 Scope（项目范围）：说明改进项目包括什么、不包括什么，避免目标扩张。
可以在 Excel 中创建解决方案选择矩阵，团队应以集思广益的方式共同处理该文档。在解决方案集思广益期间，最好让整个团队以及相关主题专家和利益相关者参与其中。这确保了团队提出的解决方案更有可能适合流程和业务；一旦使用矩阵选择解决方案，团队还可能必须获得赞助商、拥护者或领导委员会的支持，然后才能部分或全面实施。当解决方案需要支出或会影响项目范围之外的流程和人员时尤其如此。

rows: 术语说明 Stakeholder（相关方）：会影响项目或被项目影响的人或组织。
zh.text: 术语说明 Stakeholder（相关方）：会影响项目或被项目影响的人或组织。
解决方案选择矩阵示例如下图所示。
该矩阵通过以下方式完成：
1. 在第一列中输入问题陈述。 如果团队决定该陈述应该是，这应该是在测量阶段达成的最终问题陈述

收集数据后更改。否则，这可能是定义阶段的问题陈述。 2. 输入分析阶段经过优先级验证的根本原因。如果团队打算在改进阶段尝试解决多个根本原因，则应该为每个根本原因创建一个解决方案选择矩阵。 3. 在第三栏中集思广益潜在的解决方案。在头脑风暴过程中，团队不应质疑或尝试分析解决方案，而应记录任何看似可行的建议解决方案。唯一可能被忽视的解决方案是那些显然超出范围或不可能的解决方案，但领导头脑风暴活动的黑带必须运用他或她自己对此类建议的洞察力。 4. 在很高的层面上注意到可以实施解决方案的实用方法。在分析章节中使用的一个示例中，一家商业面包店因缺乏烘焙说明而出现问题。在这种情况下，六西格玛团队可能会建议向员工提供适当的指示。解决方案的实现方法是书面文档和培训。 5. 评级解决方案。创建可能的解决方案和实用方法的列表后，团队对每种可能性的有效性、可行性和成本效益进行评级。每个类别的评分在 1 到 10 之间。

a.有效性衡量解决方案消除问题根源的程度，1 表示无效，10 表示非常有效。 b.可行性是实施改进所需努力的衡量标准，1 表示由于所需的努力或资源而不可行，10 表示高度可行。 c.成本效益是对项目成本与预期节省的比较的估计指标。该评级不是正式的成本效益分析，而是高级别的估计。如果预计节省的成本超过与项目相关的成本，团队就会将该解决方案评为最高级别。否则，团队会将解决方案排名较低。 6. 将有效性、可行性和成本效益的分数相乘，计算出总分。总体得分可用于确定解决方案的优先级，并选择具有最佳总体有效性、可行性和成本效益评级的解决方案。
考虑根据分析章节中使用的医疗索赔拒绝示例创建的可能的解决方案选择矩阵。
在分析章节中，帕累托分析表明，及时提交问题是索赔被拒率高的根本原因。在上面的解决方案选择矩阵中，团队在发现由于索赔计费人员没有及时获得所有正确信息而未按时提交索赔后，确定了四种可能的解决方案并确定了优先级。

rows: 术语说明 Pareto（帕累托）：按影响大小排序，常用于找出“少数关键原因”。
zh.text: 术语说明 Pareto（帕累托）：按影响大小排序，常用于找出“少数关键原因”。
第一个解决方案是要求前台收集尽可能多的信息。该团队对该解决方案的有效性给予了中等评级，因为它没有解决索赔中仍然可能缺少临床信息的事实。但是，该过程可以有效地收集人口统计信息，并且不需要大量费用或精力来实施，因此团队对其他类别的评价很高。
接下来，团队考虑创建并需要患者电子邮件或门户系统，以便计费人员在信息丢失时可以快速与患者沟通。由于并非所有患者都有电子邮件，而且许多患者不太可能使用这样的系统，因此该团队将该解决方案的可行性和有效性评为较低。
该团队考虑的第三个解决方案是要求临床医生在就诊患者期间绘制图表时包含计费所需的所有信息。该团队认为这个解决方案相当有效，但临床医生在处理患者问题时不太可能花时间如此彻底。该解决方案还可能会减慢医生就诊的速度，从而对收入产生负面影响。
最后，团队考虑了一个解决方案，让某些工作人员负责信息缺失的索赔。由于这些工作人员可以专注于遗漏的索赔，并通过团队构建的新工作流程进行工作，因此他们更有可能按时提交索赔。总体而言，团队
该解决方案排名最高，但他们决定同时实施解决方案一和方案四，因为解决方案一只需很少的努力，并且实际上有助于推动解决方案四的成功。

en.text: When teams include the appropriate individuals in the process, a solutions selection matrix is very adept at identifying the best possible solutions for implementation. Leadership councils and executive sponsors often want more information about the costs and benefits expected for a solution, though, so teams should be prepared to create a cost benefit analysis. Black Belts often have many of the elements required for such an analysis after the measure and analyze phases, but teams might also need to work with accounting, finance, or business planning departments to gain accurate financial details required.

The goal of a cost benefits analysis is to compare the costs of implementing a solution with the monetary benefits expected from the solution. Costs include expenses such as software development or purchase, equipment purchase, building development or renovation, additional labor or hiring, training expenses, additional supplies, and any losses associated with disruption as the solution is implemented. Benefits might include an increase in product margin, increase in revenue, cost savings or avoidance, and intangible benefits such as increase to staff morale or customer retention.

Six Sigma teams usually aren’t in a position to handle detailed cost benefit analysis such as might be completed by a certified accountant, but they can create an idea of cost benefit relationships via the payback method of analysis. This is the simplest way to approach such an analysis and provides leadership with an estimated time before a project “pays off.”
Payback, or Pay Off, Analysis

To conduct a payback analysis, Six Sigma teams must have an estimated cost for the project as well as the estimated financial benefit per year. Remember, financial benefits don’t just include increases in revenue. Cost savings, new customers, or mitigation of customer loss can all be considered as contributing to benefits each year. The team also needs to understand the estimated operating costs of an improvement for each year.
The formula for this analysis is:
(Cost of implementing solution) / (Annual financial benefits – annual costs)
If a project costs $50,000 to implement and $2,000 per year in extra labor, and the team expects the project to generate $15,000 in financial benefits each year, then the calculation is:
Or, approximately 3.84 years until the project pays for itself.

Obviously, organizational leaders are looking for payback calculations that are as short as possible, but if the solution will solve a major problem or set a foundation for extraordinary success in the future, a longer payback time doesn’t necessarily keep a solution off the table.
Net Present Value

A more concise way of calculating cost benefits is known as the Net Present Value, or NPV. NPV adjusts benefits and costs as time passes because cash flow in the future is not as valuable as current cash flow due to inflation and other economic factors. The discount rate for various endeavors can be calculated using expected return, interest rates, or inflation rates. Often, corporate finance departments can provide Six Sigma teams with a discount rate used for NPV in the company. A very basic NPV model is shown below.
This year First Year Second Year Third Year Total
Benefits
Increased revenue $10,000 $20,000 $30,000
Costs
Total Benefits –
Cost Annual
NPV (Discounted
5 percent)

In this model, the team spent $50,000 implementing a solution and expects a $2,000 labor expense associated with the solution each year. The estimated benefits from the solution are recorded for the first two years, and all of the benefits and costs are added up for a third-year view. The NPV is discounted by 5 percent, and the final number is seen in the bottom right cell: $2,850. The goal is a positive NPV, so this project fits that goal.

zh.text: 当团队在流程中包含适当的个人时，解决方案选择矩阵非常擅长识别最佳的实施解决方案。不过，领导委员会和执行发起人通常希望获得有关解决方案预期成本和收益的更多信息，因此团队应该准备好创建成本收益分析。 在测量和分析阶段之后，黑带人员通常拥有此类分析所需的许多要素，但团队可能还需要与会计、财务或业务规划部门合作，以获得所需的准确财务详细信息。
成本效益分析的目标是将实施解决方案的成本与解决方案预期的货币效益进行比较。成本包括软件开发或购买、设备购买、建筑开发或翻新、额外劳动力或雇用、培训费用、额外供应品等费用，以及与解决方案实施过程中的中断相关的任何损失。好处可能包括增加产品利润、增加收入、节省或避免成本，以及无形的好处，例如提高员工士气或保留客户。
六西格玛团队通常无法处理详细的成本效益分析，例如由注册会计师完成的成本效益分析，但他们可以通过投资回收期分析法创建成本效益关系的概念。这是进行此类分析的最简单方法，并为领导提供项目“获得回报”之前的预计时间。
回报或回报分析
为了进行投资回收分析，六西格玛团队必须估算项目成本以及每年的财务收益。请记住，财务收益不仅仅包括收入的增加。节省成本、新客户或减少客户流失都可以被视为每年带来的收益。团队还需要了解每年改进的预计运营成本。
该分析的公式为：
（实施解决方案的成本）/（年度经济效益 - 年度成本）
如果一个项目的实施成本为 50,000 美元，每年的额外劳动力成本为 2,000 美元，并且团队预计该项目每年产生 15,000 美元的经济效益，则计算结果为：
或者，大约需要 3.84 年，项目才能收回成本。
显然，组织领导者正在寻求尽可能短的投资回收期计算，但如果解决方案能够解决主要问题或为未来的非凡成功奠定基础，那么较长的投资回收期并不一定会排除解决方案。
净现值
计算成本效益的一种更简洁的方法称为净现值（NPV）。 NPV 随着时间的推移调整收益和成本，因为由于通货膨胀和其他经济因素，未来的现金流不如当前的现金流有价值。各种努力的贴现率可以使用预期回报、利率或通货膨胀率来计算。通常，企业财务部门可以为六西格玛团队提供用于公司净现值的折扣率。一个非常基本的 NPV 模型如下所示。
今年 第一年 第二年 第三年 总计
好处
成本

en.text: Once a solution is selected and work done to bring it into production at a minimal level, the Six Sigma team is ready for a pilot. A pilot is a limited trial of a solution in a live environment. No matter how much analysis was completed or how well test cases were run, teams can’t know for certain how a solution
will behave in the “wild.” The live production environment will always have variables that teams can’t account for, particularly when people are involved in the process.
Benefits of a limited pilot include:
Use of resources are limited, which reduces waste if the solution turns out to be incorrect or not effective in resolving the problem
Confirmation that expected results occur
Allows troubleshooting of a new solution on a smaller scale to minimize disruption during full transition
Lets employees outside of the Six Sigma team provide feedback on the solution and implementation to make the final rollout more successful

Teams don’t have to pilot every change they make. Simple or small changes can be made without piloting as long as teams document the changes well and measure results for verification. When changes are large in scope, could cause expensive or expansive consequences, or would be difficult to reverse, teams should begin with a pilot. The same is true for any solution that might be expensive to implement at a full scale.

Pilots can occur on a limited scale or for a limited time. Limited scale pilots incorporate a specific region, team, group of people, or machinery. Limited time pilots implement a temporary change; at the end of a scheduled time, the team makes a decision about whether the change should be made permanent.

Pilots can occur with either processes or products. Process pilots might feature testing specific locations, testing results with some customers, working with some employees to test new processes, or conducting dry runs of a process without impact to the end-user. Product pilots are conducted using test markets, product models, or alpha and beta testing of the product with certain end-users.

To create a pilot, a Six Sigma team must first select the audience for the pilot. Internal process pilots can be performed by a select team or a select few employees. External product pilots can be performed using a subset of customers. For the best pilot results, avoid biasing results by selecting the best possible performers or customers who are most likely to work hard to ensure a product succeeds. When the solution is implemented on a full scale, it will be used by everyone, so you want to ensure it works for everyone.

When possible, pilot at a very small level and then expand the pilot to a larger audience. This is the premise behind alpha and beta testing. A very small set of loyal customers tests the product first, because you know they will provide feedback. Next, an expanded set of customers tests the product after teams have made changes associated with feedback from the first group of users. Finally, if the limited tests are successful, teams choose to roll out the product to the entire audience.
Analyze Pilot and Test Results

Six Sigma experts can use all the tools associated with the analyze phase to test whether solutions have a positive impact during testing or pilot programs. Hypothesis testing can be used to compare data from before the solution to data after the solution, determining if there is a statistically significant and positive change. Graphical analysis can be very helpful in demonstrating for executive leaders how a solution has positively impacted a problem by reducing defects, improving production or efficiency, or reducing costs.

zh.text: 总效益 –
每年费用
NPV（折扣
在此模型中，团队花费 50,000 美元实施解决方案，预计每年与该解决方案相关的人工费用为 2,000 美元。记录前两年该解决方案的估计收益，并将所有收益和成本相加作为第三年的视图。 NPV 折扣 5%，最终数字显示在右下方的单元格中：2,850 美元。目标是正 NPV，因此该项目符合该目标。
试点解决方案
一旦选择了解决方案并完成了将其投入生产的最低水平的工作，六西格玛团队就准备好进行试点。试点是在实际环境中对解决方案进行有限的试验。 无论完成了多少分析或测试用例运行得如何，团队都无法确定解决方案如何
会在“野外”表现。现场生产环境总是存在团队无法解释的变量，特别是当人们参与该过程时。
有限试点的好处包括：
资源的使用是有限的，如果解决方案被证明不正确或不能有效解决问题，这可以减少浪费
确认预期结果发生
允许对新解决方案进行小规模故障排除，以最大程度地减少完整过渡期间的中断
让六西格玛团队之外的员工对解决方案和实施提供反馈，使最终的部署更加成功
团队不必尝试他们所做的每一个改变。只要团队很好地记录更改并测量结果以进行验证，就可以在不进行试点的情况下进行简单或小的更改。当变更范围很大、可能导致昂贵或广泛的后果或难以逆转时，团队应该从试点开始。对于全面实施可能成本高昂的任何解决方案来说也是如此。
试点可以在有限的规模或有限的时间内进行。有限规模试点包括特定区域、团队、人员或机器。限时试点实施临时变更；在预定时间结束时，团队决定是否应将更改永久化。
试点可以针对流程或产品进行。流程试点可能会测试特定位置，与一些客户一起测试结果，与一些员工一起测试新流程，或者在不影响最终用户的情况下进行流程试运行。产品试点是通过测试市场、产品模型或与某些最终用户对产品进行 alpha 和 beta 测试来进行的。
要制作试播集，六西格玛团队必须首先选择试播集的受众。内部流程试点可以由选定的团队或选定的少数员工来执行。可以使用一部分客户来执行外部产品试点。为了获得最佳试点结果，请选择最有可能努力确保产品成功的最佳表现者或客户，以避免结果出现偏差。 当该解决方案全面实施时，它将被每个人使用，因此您需要确保它适用于每个人。
如果可能，先在很小的范围内进行试点，然后将试点范围扩大到更大的受众。这是 alpha 和 beta 测试背后的前提。一小部分忠实客户首先测试产品，因为您知道他们会提供反馈。接下来，在团队根据第一组用户的反馈进行相关更改后，更多的客户测试产品。最后，如果有限的测试成功，团队就会选择向所有受众推出该产品。
分析试点和测试结果
六西格玛专家可以使用与分析阶段相关的所有工具来测试解决方案在测试或试点项目期间是否产生积极影响。假设检验可用于比较解决方案之前的数据与解决方案之后的数据，确定是否存在统计上显着且积极的变化。图形分析非常有助于向执行领导者展示解决方案如何通过减少缺陷、提高产量或效率或降低成本来对问题产生积极影响。

rows: 术语说明 Hypothesis Test（假设检验）：用样本数据判断某个统计假设是否有足够证据被拒绝。

en.text: Once solutions have been verified through tests and analysis, teams can begin the work of implementing changes on a large scale. Teams should create and work from an action plan during this vital and active stage of the DMAIC process to ensure that no plans or requirements fall through the cracks. During the active part of the improve phase, the project leader will likely delegate numerous activities to members of the team, and the team will also rely on input and assistance from those outside of the Six Sigma team. Keeping an action plan document helps everyone on the team see where they are in the process, what they are responsible for, and what date work must be completed by.

If the Six Sigma team is working with a project manager, he or she is likely responsible for action plan documentation and follow-up. Teams can also use a basic spreadsheet or Word document, which should be saved in a common location, to keep track of what work is to be done and who will do it.
Because every project is unique, improve action plans are also unique. Most action plans will contain common tasks such as documentation, training, and transition.
Documentation

First, the Six Sigma team should have documented all of its work so far during the DMAIC process. If asked, the Black Belt or designated team member should be able to present analysis in the form of data tables, statistical calculations and explanations, and graphical analysis. All brainstorming activities and diagrams should be saved in a central file location where all team members can view them and appropriate team members can edit them as needed. Access to these documents helps team members work efficiently on additional documentation required for implementation.

Almost any organization will require new processes or changes to processes to be recorded in standard operating procedures. Depending on the organizational structure of a business, Six Sigma teams might be responsible for drafting such documents or they might need to work with knowledge management resources to create SOPs in keeping with corporate branding and templates. Teams might also create general communications letting other staff members know about the upcoming changes and the reasons for those changes as well as general reference documents such as cheat sheets and Frequently Asked Questions.
Training

Strong documentation is key to the next part of implementing improvements, which is staff training. Six Sigma teams usually aren’t in charge of complete staff training on any improvement they make. Instead, teams begin the training process by working with training subject matter experts or delegates in the department impacted by the upcoming changes. The Six Sigma team trains these individuals, who then go on to train other individuals who will be impacted by the process. At some point, the process training should be integrated into regular organizational training by trainers or knowledge management departments.
Transition

During the improve phase of a DMAIC project, teams should begin to consider the need to transition a process back to the business and traditional process owner. Transition is part of the control phase, but teams should move from improve to control with a good understanding of how the process should be
measured and monitored. Strong documentation and training during the improve phase helps cement the success of the control phase.

zh.text: 一旦解决方案通过测试和分析得到验证，团队就可以开始大规模实施变更的工作。在 DMAIC 流程的这一重要而活跃的阶段，团队应制定并执行行动计划，以确保没有任何计划或要求被遗漏。在改进阶段的积极部分，项目负责人可能会将大量活动委托给团队成员，并且团队还将依赖六西格玛团队之外的人员的投入和帮助。保留行动计划文档可以帮助团队中的每个人了解他们在流程中的位置、他们负责什么以及工作必须在什么日期之前完成。
如果六西格玛团队与项目经理合作，他或她可能负责行动计划的文件记录和后续工作。团队还可以使用基本的电子表格或 Word 文档（应保存在公共位置）来跟踪要完成的工作以及由谁来完成。
由于每个项目都是独一无二的，因此改进行动计划也是独一无二的。大多数行动计划将包含常见任务，例如文档、培训和过渡。
文档
首先，六西格玛团队应该记录 DMAIC 流程中迄今为止的所有工作。如果被要求，黑带或指定的团队成员应该能够以数据表格、统计计算和解释以及图形分析的形式进行分析。所有头脑风暴活动和图表都应保存在一个中央文件位置，所有团队成员都可以在其中查看它们，并且适当的团队成员可以根据需要对其进行编辑。访问这些文档有助于团队成员有效地处理实施所需的其他文档。
几乎所有组织都需要将新流程或流程变更记录在标准操作程序中。根据企业的组织结构，六西格玛团队可能负责起草此类文件，或者他们可能需要与知识管理资源合作来创建与企业品牌和模板保持一致的SOP。团队还可以创建一般性沟通，让其他员工了解即将发生的变化和这些变化的原因，以及一般参考文档，例如备忘单和常见问题解答。
培训
强大的文档是实施改进的下一部分（即员工培训）的关键。 六西格玛团队通常不负责对他们所做的任何改进进行完整的员工培训。相反，团队通过与受即将发生的变化影响的部门中的培训主题专家或代表合作来开始培训过程。六西格玛团队培训这些人，然后他们继续培训其他将受到该过程影响的人。在某些时候，过程培训应该被培训师或知识管理部门纳入定期的组织培训中。
过渡
在 DMAIC 项目的改进阶段，团队应开始考虑将流程移交回业务和传统流程所有者的必要性。过渡是控制阶段的一部分，但团队应该从改进转向控制，并充分了解流程应该如何进行
测量和监测。改进阶段的强大文档和培训有助于巩固控制阶段的成功。

en.text: o Solutions were reviewed and prioritized. o One or two top solutions were selected for action. o Solutions were implemented on a limited basis. o Data from limited trials was analyzed and solutions appear to work as expected. o Cost-benefits analysis was performed. o Sponsor, champion, or executive steering committee signed off on implementing the solution completely. o All team members agree the solution should be implemented. o The solution is fully documented through SOPs and training materials. o Critical staff received training on the solution and are prepared to pass that training on to others.

zh.text: o 对解决方案进行审查并确定优先级。 o 选择一两个最佳解决方案来实施。 o 解决方案的实施是有限的。 o 对有限试验的数据进行了分析，解决方案似乎按预期发挥作用。 o 进行了成本效益分析。 o 发起人、支持者或执行指导委员会签署完全实施该解决方案。 o 所有团队成员都同意应该实施该解决方案。 o 该解决方案通过 SOP 和培训材料进行了完整记录。 o 关键员工接受了有关解决方案的培训，并准备将培训传授给其他人。

en.text: The last stage of a DMAIC project is control. During the control phase, teams build monitors that let them ensure the process continues to work successfully after changes are implemented across the regular business process. At the same time, Six Sigma teams work to transition the process back to the process owner.

Up until this point in the DMAIC process, Six Sigma teams have worked with statistical analysis tools, and a Black Belt or other Six Sigma expert has been present to walk team members through analysis and interpretation. While many organizations train process owners and other employees in Six Sigma fundamentals, it isn’t always true that a process owner and his or her team will be familiar with the statistical controls that Six Sigma experts have been using. Because of that, appropriate documentation via a control plan and education regarding tools such as control charts might be necessary to ensure business teams can maintain a process and identify when it is out of control and needs remediation.

zh.text: DMAIC 项目的最后阶段是控制。在控制阶段，团队构建监视器，以确保在常规业务流程中实施变更后流程继续成功运行。与此同时，六西格玛团队致力于将流程交还给流程所有者。

rows: 术语说明 DMAIC：定义、测量、分析、改进、控制；适用于改进已有流程。
zh.text: 术语说明 DMAIC：定义、测量、分析、改进、控制；适用于改进已有流程。
到目前为止，DMAIC流程中，六西格玛团队一直在使用统计分析工具，并有黑带或其他六西格玛专家在场，带领团队成员进行分析和解读。虽然许多组织对流程所有者和其他员工进行六西格玛基础知识培训，但流程所有者及其团队并不总是熟悉六西格玛专家一直在使用的统计控制。因此，可能需要通过控制计划和有关控制图等工具的教育进行适当的记录，以确保业务团队能够维护流程并确定流程何时失控并需要补救。

rows: 术语说明 Control Chart（控制图）：用时间序列和控制界限判断过程是否处于受控状态。
zh.text: 术语说明 Control Chart（控制图）：用时间序列和控制界限判断过程是否处于受控状态。
修改FMEA

en.text: At this time, Six Sigma teams might want to revisit the FMEA tool originally introduced in chapter 13. Six Sigma teams initially use the Failure Modes and Effect Analysis to identify potential failures in a process and causes of those failures. In chapter 13, we discussed how the FMEA listed potential failure points and ranked them according to severity, occurrence, and detection, calculating a total risk priority number.

At the end of the improve phase or beginning of the control phase, Six Sigma teams should revisit the FMEA, noting what recommended actions were completed and recalculating risk priority numbers for the improved process. There are two reasons for revisiting the FMEA. First, the team is able to see that positive and significant changes have occurred because of the solutions adapted during the improve phase. For any root cause that matched a solution implemented, the team would hope to see a smaller risk priority number.

Second, an updated FMEA helps the team identify the next problem or root cause that might be addressed. Remember, Six Sigma is a continuous improvement initiative. The team might have implemented a solution and met an improvement goal, but further improvements can always be made. Control is a time to review the process and suggest possible improvements for future projects.

rows: 术语说明 FMEA：失效模式与影响分析，用于提前识别风险、影响和控制措施。
zh.text: 术语说明 FMEA：失效模式与影响分析，用于提前识别风险、影响和控制措施。
此时，六西格玛团队可能需要重新审视最初在第13章中介绍的FMEA工具。六西格玛团队最初使用故障模式和影响分析来识别过程中的潜在故障以及这些故障的原因。在第13章中，我们讨论了FMEA如何列出潜在的故障点，并根据严重程度、发生情况和检测情况对它们进行排序，计算总风险优先级数。
在改进阶段结束或控制阶段开始时，六西格玛团队应重新审视 FMEA，记录已完成的建议行动，并重新计算改进过程的风险优先级编号。重新审视 FMEA 有两个原因。首先，团队能够看到由于改进阶段采用的解决方案而发生了积极且重大的变化。对于与所实施的解决方案相匹配的任何根本原因，团队希望看到更小的风险优先级数字。

rows: 术语说明 Root Cause（根本原因）：真正驱动问题发生的原因；解决它才能防止问题反复出现。
zh.text: 术语说明 Root Cause（根本原因）：真正驱动问题发生的原因；解决它才能防止问题反复出现。
其次，更新的 FMEA 可以帮助团队识别下一个可能需要解决的问题或根本原因。请记住，六西格玛是一项持续改进的举措。团队可能已经实施了解决方案并达到了改进目标，但始终可以进行进一步的改进。控制是审查流程并为未来项目提出可能的改进建议的时间。

en.text: To facilitate continued success, Six Sigma teams should create a written control plan for the process owner. The purpose of a control plan is to help the process owner and business team track and respond to key performance indicators so that the process remains improved. The control plan should be a concise, easy-to-reference document that tells the business team when to monitor, how to monitor, what range of data is acceptable to the monitor, and how to respond with corrective action if the range measured is not acceptable.
Control plans can be spreadsheets, specialized digital documents, or hard-copy documents posted at a work station. Common elements of a control plan include:
Company, division, or department name
Name of person who created the plan
Date the plan was created
Name of the person who last edited the plan
Date the plan was last edited
Project and/or process name or identifier
Process owner
List of process steps where control action is required
CTQ or metric associated with each action required
Limit specifications, or the acceptable range of measurements
The unit of measurement
The method of measurement
The necessary sample size
The frequency of measurement
The person responsible for measurement
Where the information is recorded
Correction actions
Associated policy and procedure documents

In discussing quality in chapter 8, we introduced the example of a company that makes chocolate bars and noted that the amount of sugar in the chocolate bar recipe was critical to the customer’s experience with the end product. If a Six Sigma team were tasked with improving customer satisfaction with a new chocolate bar product, they might have implemented a solution that ensures the proper amount of sugar is added to the mixture at the right temperature to incorporate the ingredient appropriately.
A control plan for the new chocolate bar solution might look something like the document below.
Company: XYZ Sweets
Control Plan Created by: Joe Black Belt
Process: Sugar addition, raw goods mixture
Control Plan Created on: Jan. 4, 2012
Process Owner: Sue Processor
Process Step
Addition of sugar to batch Heating of batch
CTQ/Metric
Total amount added to batch Mean temperature during mixing
Limit specification LSL: 4.90 cups USL: 5.10 cups
LSL: 105 F
USL: 110 F
Unit of measurement Cups Degrees F
Method of measurement 6-cup sugar test bowl Read integrated digital thermometer on mixing machine
Sample Size
One batch 3 reading, 2 minutes apart, during mixing
Frequency Every 2 hours Every 2 hours
Employee
Mixer operator Mixer Operator
Record data in Mix operation log spreadsheet Mix operation log spreadsheet

Corrective action Manually measure correct amount for current batch to allow for processing, calibrate sugar disbursement machine following SOP 100.54, test sugar disbursement for first batch after calibration to ensure problem is resolved. Report issue to supervisor.
Turn off machine, waste inappropriately heated batch, and report temperature calibration issue to maintenance.

The above example control plan provides instructions for two specific steps in the process with easy-to- understand measurement and monitor requirements. To reduce the chance of errors, the Six Sigma team has even specified a special measuring tool for measuring the sugar in the test batch so that every operator performing the monitor measures using the same tool.

At the end of the control document, the team provides steps for corrective action. The first step can be corrected by the operator, who has the ability to calibrate the machine him or herself. The temperature calibration in this case can’t be performed by the operator, which means the process has to be stopped so that someone can attend to the issue. Note that it is always preferable, when possible, to build corrective action at the process level, such as was done with the sugar measurement. This minimizes downtime, puts employees more in control of the processes they own, and helps employees stay involved with the quality process.

The control plan above assumes that manual measurements must be taken or recorded. Optimally, Six Sigma teams should look for ways to automate measurements, which means data can be continuously gathered and converted into statistical process controls such as control charts. Automated data gathering doesn’t mean a control plan isn’t necessary, it just means that a control plan won’t include instructions for gathering the data. Instead, employees and process owners can be instructed to review automated data or control charts and take action if necessary.

You’ll also note that the specification requirements given above are provided with LSL and USL. LSL is the lower specification limit and the USL is the upper specification limit. These are the upper and lower limits of the acceptable range.

zh.text: 为了促进持续成功，六西格玛团队应为流程所有者制定书面控制计划。控制计划的目的是帮助流程所有者和业务团队跟踪和响应关键绩效指标，以便流程不断得到改进。控制计划应该是一份简洁、易于参考的文档，告诉业务团队何时进行监控、如何监控、监控者可接受的数据范围以及如果测量的范围不可接受时如何采取纠正措施。
控制计划可以是电子表格、专门的数字文档或张贴在工作站上的硬拷贝文档。控制计划的常见要素包括：
公司、部门或部门名称
创建该计划的人的姓名
计划创建日期
最后编辑该计划的人员姓名
上次编辑计划的日期
项目和/或流程名称或标识符
流程所有者
需要控制操作的过程步骤列表
与所需的每项操作相关的 CTQ 或指标

rows: 术语说明 CTQ（关键质量特性）：把客户需求转化为可测量的质量要求。
zh.text: 术语说明 CTQ（关键质量特性）：把客户需求转化为可测量的质量要求。
限制规格或可接受的测量范围
计量单位
测量方法
必要的样本量

rows: 术语说明 Sample / Sampling（样本/抽样）：从总体中选取部分数据进行分析；样本应尽量代表总体。
zh.text: 术语说明 Sample / Sampling（样本/抽样）：从总体中选取部分数据进行分析；样本应尽量代表总体。
测量频率
测量负责人
信息记录在哪里
纠正措施
相关政策和程序文件
在第 8 章讨论质量时，我们介绍了一家生产巧克力棒的公司的例子，并指出巧克力棒配方中的糖含量对于客户对最终产品的体验至关重要。如果六西格玛团队的任务是提高客户对新巧克力棒产品的满意度，他们可能会实施一种解决方案，确保在正确的温度下将适量的糖添加到混合物中，以适当地混合成分。
新巧克力棒解决方案的控制计划可能类似于下面的文档。
公司：XYZ糖果
控制计划创建者：Joe 黑带
工艺：加糖、原料混合
控制计划创建于：一月 2012年4月
进程所有者：Sue Processor
工艺步骤
向批次中添加糖 加热批次
CTQ/公制
添加到批次中的总量 混合期间的平均温度
极限规格 LSL：4.90 杯 USL：5.10 杯
LSL：105 华氏度
上层高度：110 华氏度
测量单位 杯 度 F
测量方法 6 杯糖测试碗 读取搅拌机上的集成数字温度计
样本量
一批 3 次读数，间隔 2 分钟，混合期间
频率 每 2 小时 每 2 小时
员工
混合器操作员 混合器操作员
在混合操作日志电子表格中记录数据 混合操作日志电子表格
纠正措施 手动测量当前批次的正确数量以进行加工，按照 SOP 100.54 校准糖分配机，在校准后测试第一批糖的分配，以确保问题得到解决。向主管报告问题。
关闭机器，浪费加热不当的批次，并向维护人员报告温度校准问题。
上述示例控制计划提供了流程中两个特定步骤的说明，以及易于理解的测量和监控要求。为了减少出错的机会，六西格玛团队甚至指定了一种特殊的测量工具来测量测试批次中的糖分，以便每个进行监测的操作人员都使用相同的工具进行测量。
在控制文档的末尾，团队提供了纠正措施的步骤。第一步可以由操作员纠正，操作员有能力自己校准机器。在这种情况下，操作员无法执行温度校准，这意味着必须停止该过程，以便有人可以处理该问题。请注意，如果可能的话，最好在过程层面采取纠正措施，例如糖测量。这可以最大限度地减少停机时间，让员工更好地控制自己的流程，并帮助员工继续参与质量流程。
上述控制计划假设必须进行或记录手动测量。 最理想的情况是，六西格玛团队应该寻找自动化测量的方法，这意味着可以不断收集数据并将其转换为统计过程控制，例如控制图。自动数据收集并不意味着不需要控制计划，它只是意味着控制计划不会包含收集数据的说明。相反，可以指示员工和流程所有者查看自动化数据或控制图，并在必要时采取行动。
您还会注意到，LSL 和 USL 提供了上面给出的规格要求。 LSL 是规格下限，USL 是规格上限。这些是可接受范围的上限和下限。

en.text: In addition to providing a control plan, Six Sigma teams can implement specific visual controls in a workplace to help business teams maintain a controlled process. Some of these tools were covered in chapter 4 on Lean process management, including 5S. Other visual controls teams might implement include signs, posted matrixes and instructions, auditing boards that let teams keep track of individual or group performance over time, color coding, and safety signals.

Standard operating procedures can often be distilled to visual representations on posters. A coffee shop, for example, might provide employees with a visual representation of what ingredients are used to create various complex drink flavors. Such a poster ensures that employees can prepare drinks quickly while reducing errors in ingredient inclusion.

Other SOP visualizations might include safety procedures in a medical environment, such as visual reminders for hand washing and short pictorial representations for how to operate equipment such as hospital beds. In an office environment, pictorial instructions are found on copy machines, where pictures indicate how paper should be loaded and visual gifs are often displayed on LED screens to help employees remove jammed paper. These are some examples that Six Sigma teams can follow when creating documents that will help business staff accept ownership of an improved process and maintain the improvements made.

zh.text: 除了提供控制计划外，六西格玛团队还可以在工作场所实施特定的可视化控制，帮助业务团队维持受控流程。其中一些工具在关于精益流程管理的第 4 章中进行了介绍，包括 5S。其他视觉控制团队可能实施的措施包括标志、张贴的矩阵和说明、让团队跟踪个人或小组随时间变化的表现的审核板、颜色编码和安全信号。
标准操作程序通常可以被提炼为海报上的视觉表现形式。例如，咖啡店可能会为员工提供用于创建各种复杂饮料口味的成分的可视化表示。这样的海报确保员工可以快速准备饮料，同时减少配料错误。
其他 SOP 可视化可能包括医疗环境中的安全程序，例如洗手的视觉提醒以及如何操作医院病床等设备的简短图示。在办公环境中，复印机上会出现图形说明，其中图片指示如何加载纸张，并且 LED 屏幕上通常会显示视觉 GIF，以帮助员工清除卡纸。这些是六西格玛团队在创建文档时可以遵循的一些示例，这些文档将帮助业务人员接受改进流程的所有权并维护所做的改进。

en.text: One of the most common methods Six Sigma teams use to monitor a process is the control chart. A number of types of control charts exist, and Six Sigma experts must choose the right control chart for the type of data and analytical purpose.
Now, we’ll cover the visual tests that let a Six Sigma team or process owner know that a process is out of control.
A basic control chart has the following elements:
A line chart of data with plot points for specific data points
An x-bar line representing the average of the data points
Lines above and below the x-bar line representing 1, 2, and 3 standard deviations from the median in either direction
An upper control limit (UCL) line at 3 standard deviations above the median
A lower control limit (LCL) line at 3 standard deviations below the median

Above is an example of a control chart. The middle line, which is black, is the x-bar line. The x-bar line is bounded by green lines on both sides, indicating 1 and 2 standard deviations away from the mid-line. Those lines are bounded by orange lines on either side: at the top, the upper control limit, and on bottom, the lower control limit. Between the various lines are areas of the control chart, designated as C, B, and A going in either direction. These distinct areas are important for understanding if a process might be out of control. They are also called Zones 1, 2, and 3.

A control chart is best displayed using an automated reporting system or dashboard, where process owners or responsible employees can view it as needed. If automated data collection and control charting is not possible, then a business analyst can be tasked with collecting data and presenting it in this format periodically, though periodic graphical analysis is less likely to catch a problem of control within a process.
Statistical Process Control Tests: Control Charts
Eight tests exist that can quickly tell someone viewing a control chart if a process is out of control.

Test one: A single point on the control chart appears outside of the upper or lower control limits. If this occurs, process owners should take immediate action, because it is evidence of a major problem within a process. While there is a very remote possibility that shifts outside of three standard deviations can occur randomly, the likelihood is only 3 in 1,000.

Test two: Nine points in a row appear on one side of the center line. This indicates that a change occurred in the process; if the process owner knows what change caused the shift and it was intentional change, nothing needs to be done and the control chart will right itself over time with the new data. Otherwise, the process owner should investigate the process.

Test three: Six points on the control chart increase or decrease in a row, indicating the process is becoming less or more efficient or is generating fewer or more errors. Process owners should investigate unless there is a known reason for the trend.
Test four: Fourteen points on the control chart in a row alternate moving up and down. This could indicate variation in machines, employees, shifts, or over correction.
Test five: Two out of three points in a row on the control chart are in the upper A section or in the lower A section. This might indicate some type of special cause creating sudden high variation.

Test six: Four out of five points in a row on the control chart are located in the upper B section (or beyond) or the lower B section (or beyond). This can indicate a problem of major causation or a shift problem similar to that of test four.

Test seven: Fifteen points in a row are located within the C section above or below the centerline. This can indicate that control limits are no longer relevant to the process; if a team has improved variation of a process, they should recalculate control limits to new parameters. Alternatively, this might occur temporarily when short-term variation is high or low relative to the rest of the points on a control chart.

Test eight: Eight points in a row on the control chart are located on either side of the center line, but none are located in the C section above or below the line. This could indicate an issue of mixed resources or processes; a team might think they are measuring a single process when they are actually measuring two process, for example. Alternatively, it could indicate a major difference in processing for two employees or teams.

When it’s possible to create control charts and display current data on a regular basis, these charts make a good addition to a control plan. Individuals don’t have to be well-versed in statistical process control to learn about the eight tests, and business teams with the benefit of control charts can spend more time working on production or correcting issues and less time collecting and documenting measurements.
Control Versus Capability

It’s important to note the distinction between a controlled process and one that is capable of meeting customer requirements. We touched on this concept in unit 1: controlled processes don’t have a lot of variation. Capable processes don’t have a lot of variation and the outputs center around a customer requirement. This is why both control limits and specification limits are important.

Consider the example used earlier in this chapter about sugar in the chocolate mix. The specification limits ranged from 4.9 to 5.1 cups of sugar in each batch. It’s possible for a control chart to show that the process is in control if the measurements range from 3.5 to 3.6 cups of sugar per batch, but the process owner should know that those measurements aren’t going to contribute to a product that meets critical to customer quality requirements.
To understand how a process is performing against specification limits, Six Sigma teams can calculate sigma level and process capability.
Sigma Level

Sigma level is the number of standard deviations between the current process center, as measured by the median, and the nearest specification limit (not control limit.) The equation for sigma level is the smaller of the following calculations:
𝑈𝑆𝐿−𝑥̅
𝜎 𝑜𝑟 𝐿𝑆𝐿−𝑥̅
𝜎
For example, if a process has an USL of 5 and a LSL of 3, a standard deviation of .25 and a median of 4.2, then you would calculate from the USL, since the median is closer to the USL.
.25 = 3.2 = sigma level
Process Capability
Process capability is calculated by dividing the sigma level by 3. In the case of the example, the capability is 1.06.

Process capability is denoted as Cpk. A process capability of 1.33 is equal to a sigma level of 4, which is what most experts agree is the minimal level at which most customers will be satisfied. Under statistical process control, many organizations aim for a process capability of 2.0 with minimal acceptable process capability at 1.5.

rows: 术语说明 SPC（统计过程控制）：用统计方法监控过程表现，区分普通波动和异常信号。
zh.text: 术语说明 SPC（统计过程控制）：用统计方法监控过程表现，区分普通波动和异常信号。
六西格玛团队监控流程最常用的方法之一是控制图。 控制图有多种类型，六西格玛专家必须根据数据类型和分析目的选择正确的控制图。
现在，我们将介绍让六西格玛团队或流程所有者知道流程失控的可视化测试。
基本控制图具有以下元素：
带有特定数据点绘图点的数据折线图
代表数据点平均值的 X 条线

rows: 术语说明 X-Bar Chart（均值图）：监控样本均值随时间变化的控制图。
zh.text: 术语说明 X-Bar Chart（均值图）：监控样本均值随时间变化的控制图。
x 条线上方和下方的线代表任一方向距中位数 1、2 和 3 个标准差
控制上限 (UCL) 线高于中位数 3 个标准差
控制下限 (LCL) 线低于中值 3 个标准差
上面是控制图的示例。中间的黑色线是 x 条线。 x 条线两侧以绿线为界，表示距中线 1 和 2 个标准差。这些线的两侧以橙色线为界：顶部为控制上限，底部为控制下限。各条线之间是控制图的区域，指定为任一方向的 C、B 和 A。这些不同的区域对于了解流程是否可能失控非常重要。它们也称为 1 区、2 区和 3 区。
控制图最好使用自动报告系统或仪表板来显示，流程所有者或负责的员工可以根据需要查看它。如果无法实现自动数据收集和控制图表，则业务分析师可以负责收集数据并定期以这种格式呈现数据，尽管定期图形分析不太可能发现流程中的控制问题。
统计过程控制测试：控制图
存在八种测试，可以快速告诉查看控制图的人流程是否失控。
测试一：控制图上的单个点出现在控制上限或下限之外。如果发生这种情况，流程所有者应立即采取行动，因为这是流程中存在重大问题的证据。虽然随机发生超出三个标准偏差的可能性非常小，但这种可能性仅为千分之三。
测试二：连续九个点出现在中心线的一侧。 这表明流程发生了变化；如果流程所有者知道是什么变化导致了这种转变，并且这是有意的变化，则无需执行任何操作，并且控制图将随着时间的推移使用新数据自行纠正。否则，流程所有者应调查该流程。
测试三：控制图上的六个点连续增加或减少，表明过程的效率正在变得更低或更高，或者产生的错误更少或更多。除非有已知的趋势原因，否则流程所有者应该进行调查。
测试四：控制图上的十四个点连续交替上下移动。这可能表明机器、员工、轮班的变化或过度修正。
测试五：控制图上连续的三个点中有两个位于上 A 部分或下 A 部分。这可能表明某种类型的特殊原因造成突然的高变化。
测试六：控制图上连续的五个点中有四个位于上 B 部分（或之外）或下 B 部分（或之外）。这可能表明存在重大因果关系问题或类似于测试四的转移问题。
测试七：连续十五个点位于中心线上方或下方的 C 部分内。这可以表明控制限值不再与过程相关；如果团队改进了过程的变化，他们应该重新计算新参数的控制限制。或者，当短期变化相对于控制图上的其余点较高或较低时，这种情况可能会暂时发生。
测试八：控制图上连续八个点位于中心线的两侧，但没有一个点位于中心线上方或下方的 C 部分。这可能表明存在混合资源或流程的问题；例如，团队可能认为他们正在测量单个流程，但实际上他们正在测量两个流程。或者，它可能表明两个员工或团队的处理存在重大差异。
当可以创建控制图并定期显示当前数据时，这些图表可以很好地补充控制计划。 个人无需精通统计过程控制即可了解八项测试，而借助控制图的业务团队可以将更多时间用于生产或纠正问题，并减少收集和记录测量结果的时间。
控制与能力
重要的是要注意受控流程和能够满足客户要求的流程之间的区别。我们在第一单元中谈到了这个概念：受控过程没有太多变化。有效的流程没有太多变化，并且输出以客户需求为中心。这就是控制限和规格限都很重要的原因。
考虑本章前面使用的有关巧克力混合物中糖的示例。每批次的规格限制范围为 4.9 至 5.1 杯糖。如果每批次的测量范围为 3.5 至 3.6 杯糖，则控制图可以显示过程处于受控状态，但流程负责人应该知道这些测量结果不会有助于生产出满足客户关键质量要求的产品。
为了了解流程在规格限制下的执行情况，六西格玛团队可以计算西格玛水平和流程能力。

rows: 术语说明 Sigma level（西格玛水平）：衡量过程缺陷率的尺度；水平越高，单位机会中产生缺陷的概率越低。
zh.text: 术语说明 Sigma level（西格玛水平）：衡量过程缺陷率的尺度；水平越高，单位机会中产生缺陷的概率越低。

rows: 术语说明 Process Capability（过程能力）：过程稳定满足规格要求的能力，常用 Cp、Cpk 等指标描述。
zh.text: 术语说明 Process Capability（过程能力）：过程稳定满足规格要求的能力，常用 Cp、Cpk 等指标描述。
西格玛水平
西格玛水平是当前过程中心（按中值测量）与最近的规格限（不是控制限）之间的标准偏差数。西格玛水平的方程是以下计算中较小的一个：
𝑈𝑆𝐿−𝑥̅
𝜎 𝑜𝑟 𝐿𝑆𝐿−𝑥̅
𝜎
例如，如果某个流程的 USL 为 5，LSL 为 3，标准差为 0.25，中位数为 4.2，那么您将根据 USL 进行计算，因为中位数更接近 USL。

rows: 术语说明 Standard Deviation（标准差）：衡量数据离散程度的统计量；标准差越大，波动越明显。
zh.text: 术语说明 Standard Deviation（标准差）：衡量数据离散程度的统计量；标准差越大，波动越明显。
制程能力
过程能力是通过将西格玛水平除以 3 来计算的。在本例中，能力为 1.06。
过程能力表示为Cpk。 1.33 的过程能力等于 4 的西格玛水平，这是大多数专家认为大多数客户满意的最低水平。在统计过程控制下，许多组织的目标是过程能力达到 2.0，可接受的最低过程能力为 1.5。

en.text: When Six Sigma teams deem improvements and the related process to be capable and in control, and they’ve passed those processes back to business and process owner control, they should take time to celebrate and reflect on the outcome of the project. This is usually done following the final tollgate review with a sponsor or champion, and can be a quick meeting to close loose ends, recognize the work done by the team, and discuss lessons learned within the process.

The celebration and reflection meeting is also a great time for team members to bring up ideas for possible improvement projects. While improvements – and the related problems and causes – are still fresh on the team’s minds, they can effectively brainstorm ideas for next steps. As with any brainstorming session, no idea should be automatically held off the table because it seems silly, would be too hard to implement, is too costly, or seems too big. Not all of the ideas will become future projects, but the team’s input provides valuable information that the Black Belt can later share with Six Sigma leadership panels.

zh.text: 当六西格玛团队认为改进和相关流程是有能力且可控的，并且他们已将这些流程交回给业务和流程所有者控制时，他们应该花时间庆祝和反思项目的结果。这通常是在与赞助商或支持者进行最终收费站审查之后完成的，并且可以是一次快速会议，以结束未解决的问题，认可团队所做的工作，并讨论在此过程中吸取的经验教训。
庆祝和反思会议也是团队成员提出可能的改进项目想法的好时机。虽然改进以及相关的问题和原因对团队来说仍然记忆犹新，但他们可以有效地集思广益，为下一步采取行动。与任何头脑风暴会议一样，任何想法都不应因为看起来愚蠢、实施起来太困难、成本太高或看起来太大而自动被搁置。并非所有的想法都会成为未来的项目，但团队的投入提供了有价值的信息，黑带随后可以与六西格玛领导小组分享。

en.text: o The team has calculated the performance and capability of the new process o The team has written a control plan and communicated it to the process owner o The team has created a monitor for the process, either through procedures for manual data collection or automated generation of control charts o The team has provided the process owner and business team with all tools and information required to maintain improvements o The sponsor, champion, or executive leadership has been informed about the state of the improvements o The team met to reflect on the project and generate a list of ideas for future improvements
Unit 4: Beginner Statistics

zh.text: o 团队计算了新流程的绩效和能力 o 团队编写了控制计划并将其传达给流程所有者 o 团队通过手动数据收集程序或自动生成控制图为流程创建了监控器 o 团队为流程所有者和业务团队提供了维持改进所需的所有工具和信息 o 发起人、拥护者或执行领导层已获悉改进的状态 o 团队开会反思项目并生成未来改进的想法列表
第 4 单元：初级统计

title.zh: 第 17 章：中级图形分析

source.extraction: python-docx body-order extraction; source-TOC sectionized bilingual content

en.text: Graphical analysis is a critical part of the Six Sigma approach. Whenever data or ideas can be displayed in a visual format, obvious data trends can be quickly identified and communicated. Visual analysis is extremely helpful when presenting ideas to auxiliary staff or executives, who might not be trained in intermediate or advanced statistics. The Black Belt or other Six Sigma experts have the ability to tell the same story that statistics tell, but in a format that can be understood by anyone. Visual depictions are tools that make it easier for Six Sigma professionals or sponsors to retell the story to others, which can be helpful for training, building cultural buy-in for a process change, or even resource requests.
Throughout the first three units of this book, we’ve discussed a number of graphical analysis tools, including:
Pareto charts in chapters 5 and 14
Run charts in chapter 13
Box plots in chapter 14
Introduction to control charts in chapter 16
Introduction to scatter diagrams in chapter 14

In this chapter, we’ll discuss additional graphical analysis tools, including bar charts and pie charts. We’ll also look again at scatter diagrams and how to create them in Excel. In Chapter 16, you learned about the components of an X-bar control chart and how to tell if a control chart indicates a process might be out of control. In this chapter, we’ll walk through the steps for manually creating an X-bar control chart in Excel. Finally, you’ll learn how to install a data analysis add-in for various versions of Excel. The add-in provides functionality that will become relevant in the next few chapters.

zh.text: 图形分析是六西格玛方法的关键部分。只要能够以可视化格式显示数据或想法，就可以快速识别和传达明显的数据趋势。当向可能没有接受过中级或高级统计培训的辅助人员或管理人员展示想法时，可视化分析非常有用。黑带或其他六西格玛专家有能力讲述与统计数据相同的故事，但以任何人都可以理解的格式。视觉描述是让六西格玛专业人士或赞助商更容易向其他人复述故事的工具，这有助于培训、建立流程变革的文化认同，甚至资源请求。
在本书的前三个单元中，我们讨论了许多图形分析工具，包括：
第 5 章和第 14 章中的帕累托图

rows: 术语说明 Pareto（帕累托）：按影响大小排序，常用于找出“少数关键原因”。
zh.text: 术语说明 Pareto（帕累托）：按影响大小排序，常用于找出“少数关键原因”。
运行第 13 章中的图表
第 14 章的箱线图
第 16 章控制图简介

rows: 术语说明 Control Chart（控制图）：用时间序列和控制界限判断过程是否处于受控状态。
zh.text: 术语说明 Control Chart（控制图）：用时间序列和控制界限判断过程是否处于受控状态。
第14章散点图介绍
在本章中，我们将讨论其他图形分析工具，包括条形图和饼图。我们还将再次查看散点图以及如何在 Excel 中创建它们。在第 16 章中，您了解了 X 形控制图的组成部分以及如何判断控制图是否表明流程可能失控。在本章中，我们将逐步完成在 Excel 中手动创建 X 条形控制图的步骤。最后，您将学习如何为各种版本的 Excel 安装数据分析加载项。该插件提供的功能将在接下来的几章中变得相关。

rows: 术语说明 X-Bar Chart（均值图）：监控样本均值随时间变化的控制图。
zh.text: 术语说明 X-Bar Chart（均值图）：监控样本均值随时间变化的控制图。
附加图形分析工具

en.text: As with many other areas of the Six Sigma methodology, it can be easy to go overboard when dealing with graphical analysis – particularly when presenting information to others. With so many tools at your disposal, it’s tempting to pick the graph that is new, exciting, or more complex. Often, though, that means spending non-value added time creating one visual tool when an easier tool would do the job. If you recall from chapter 4, this would be an instance of over-processing to avoid.
Consider the two charts below to understand when over-processing might be occurring with regard to graphical analysis.

Both charts are a graphical representation of the same information. The first is a simple bar chart that was created within a few clicks in Excel. The second is a Pareto chart, also created in Excel but requiring many more clicks of the mouse. In this particular case, a Pareto chart is not necessary to convey the required information. The data is diverse enough that anyone can see from looking at the bar chart that sales person E accounts for a lot of the errors. In this particular case, a Pareto chart is overselling the conclusion and doesn’t need to be created if extra work is required.

In this example, it’s possible that a visual portrayal of the data isn’t required at all: the conclusion is obvious. Looking at the raw data below, you can quickly see that employee E has triple the errors as the next-highest employee.
